chapter_context.summary: The serpent deceives Eve; Adam and Eve eat the forbidden fruit. Sin enters the world, and God announces judgments and the first promise of a redeemer.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Temptation and Fall (3:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Genesis.
This section focuses on Confrontation and Judgment (3:8-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Genesis.
This section focuses on Expulsion from the Garden (3:20-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Genesis.

key_themes.title: Sovereign Creation
key_themes.description: God's absolute authority and power in bringing the universe and life into existence.

key_themes.title: The Fall and Redemption
key_themes.description: Humanity's rebellion and God's initial promise of ultimate redemption.

devotional.question: What does Genesis 3 teach you about God's faithfulness to His promises despite human failures?
How can you apply the lessons of faith and obedience from this chapter in your life today?

cultural_historical.title: Primeval Context
cultural_historical.text: Chapters 1-11 explain the origins of the cosmos, humanity, sin, and nations, setting the stage for God's covenant plan.
